one_time_description: Gain on investments reported in revenue for fiscal year 2021
economic_notes: OCN has investments in cannabis, food and beverage, casino and transportation industries. Portfolio investments include publicly traded equities: High Tide Inc. (HITI), Alternate Health Corp. (AHG), IM Cannabis Corp. (IMCC), Aequus Pharmaceuticals (AQS). Business entities include Kikiwak Inn, OCN Shell, Sports Traders, OCN Smart Farm, Aseneskak Casino, The Pas IGA, Otineka Development Corporation, Paskwayak Business Development Corporation, and several limited partnerships including Pinesiw Energy.
notes: Fiscal year 2021. Large gain on investments of $8,073,470 is a significant non-recurring item. OCN has a complex consolidation structure: 7 programs/entities fully consolidated (OCN Operating Fund, Education Services Inc., Health Authority, Housing Authority Ltd., Rod McGillivary Memorial Care Home, Land Tax, Nakow Mining and Supply Ltd.) and 13 business entities accounted for using the modified equity method. Financial year end for most incorporated entities is December 31, 2020 (not March 31, 2021). Loans receivable include $13M to Meta Growth Corp (cannabis) and prior year had $30.5M to Cresco Labs (cannabis). Significant interim financing from FNFA ($27.765M in prior year, converted to long-term). Construction in progress of $8,848,033 not included in TCA net book value. Net financial assets figure taken from Statement of Changes in Net Assets. ISC funding per statement of operations; total government transfers include ISC ($49,702,335) + FNIH ($11,363,925) + ESDC ($1,512,016) + CMHC ($646,980) + NRC ($46,914) + Public Safety Canada ($460,066) + Province of Manitoba ($3,326,515) + AMC ($1,046,661) + MKO ($1,374,945) less eliminations allocated proportionally — approximate total shown. OSR computed as total revenue minus estimated government transfers.
